     The Numbers Business Report

Five Feet Apart (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $45,729,221Details
International Box Office $46,824,988
Worldwide Box Office $92,554,209
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,472,407
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,412,981
Total $3,885,388
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Stella Grant is every bit a seventeen-year-old… she’s attached to her laptop and loves her best friends. But unlike most teenagers, she spends much of her time living in a hospital as a cystic fibrosis patient. Her life is full of routines, boundaries and self-control—all of which is put to the test when she meets an impossibly charming fellow CF patient named Will Newman. There’s an instant flirtation, though restrictions dictate that they must maintain a safe distance between them. As their connection intensifies, so does the temptation to throw the rules out the window and embrace that attraction. Further complicating matters is Will’s potentially dangerous rebellion against his ongoing medical treatment. Stella gradually inspires Will to live life to the fullest, but can she ultimately save the person she loves when even a single touch is off limits?

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$13,190,286 (28.8% of total gross)
Legs:3.47 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:49.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$7,000,000 (worldwide box office is 13.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,803 opening theaters/2,866 max. theaters, 4.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $59,707,585

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 15th, 2019 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: March 13th, 2019 (Wide) (West Indies)
March 14th, 2019 (Wide) (Argentina)
March 14th, 2019 (Wide) (Lebanon)
March 14th, 2019 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
March 14th, 2019 (Wide) (Aruba)
Video Release: May 24th, 2019 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic elements, language and suggestive material.
(Rating bulletin 2559 (Cert #51947), 1/2/2019)
Running Time: 116 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Actors Making Their Directorial Debut
Plot point: Coming of Age, Medical and Hospitals, Romance, Terminal Illness
Source material: Young Adult Book Adaptation
Subgenre: Romantic Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: CBS Films, Welle Entertainment, Wayfare Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Stella Grant is every bit a seventeen-year-old… she’s attached to her laptop and loves her best friends. But unlike most teenagers, she spends much of her time living in a hospital as a cystic fibrosis patient. Her life is full of routines, boundaries and self-control—all of which is put to the test when she meets an impossibly charming fellow CF patient named Will Newman. There’s an instant flirtation, though restrictions dictate that they must maintain a safe distance between them. As their connection intensifies, so does the temptation to throw the rules out the window and embrace that attraction. Further complicating matters is Will’s potentially dangerous rebellion against his ongoing medical treatment. Stella gradually inspires Will to live life to the fullest, but can she ultimately save the person she loves when even a single touch is off limits?
